articles.title: 3 Micro-Resets for Anxiety You Can Do Anywhere
articles.excerpt: Short, science-backed movements and breaths you can use anytime rising anxiety starts to take over.
articles.content: When anxiety spikes in the middle of your day, you rarely have time for a full class. In this guide, you’ll learn three micro-resets that take less than two minutes each: a grounding foot scan you can do at your desk, a box-breathing variation that fits into a meeting break, and a simple shoulder and neck release you can practice while waiting for your coffee. Each reset is designed to interrupt the anxiety spiral, bring attention back into the body, and signal safety to your nervous system...

articles.title: Evening Body Scan to Ease Anxiety Before Bed
articles.excerpt: Use this 10-minute body scan routine to unwind lingering anxiety and prepare your mind for rest.
articles.content: Anxiety rarely clocks out when your workday ends. In fact, many people notice their thoughts speeding up as soon as things get quiet. This guided body scan sequence is designed to meet you at that moment. Over 10 minutes, you’ll move through gentle stretches, stillness, and breath cues that help your nervous system downshift from alert to at-ease. We’ll start at the feet, work slowly up to the jaw and eyes, and finish with a simple breathing pattern you can repeat in bed...

articles.title: Sleep Rituals: A 10-Minute Wind-Down Stretch
articles.excerpt: A gentle floor-based routine to signal to your body that it’s safe to fall asleep.
articles.content: Creating a consistent wind-down ritual trains your body to recognize when it’s time to rest. This guide walks you through a 10-minute, low-effort stretching sequence you can do beside your bed. We’ll start with a supported child’s pose, move into a reclining twist, and finish with legs-up-the-wall for nervous system regulation. You’ll also learn how to pair each shape with simple breath counts to deepen the relaxation response...

articles.title: Waking Up Calm: Gentle Movement for Better Sleep Cycles
articles.excerpt: What you do in the first 15 minutes of your day might matter more for sleep than what you do at night.
articles.content: Improving sleep isn’t only about winding down. Morning light exposure, gentle movement, and a consistent wake time all support your circadian rhythm. In this article, we explore a short, 15-minute mindful movement routine you can practice after waking. The sequence combines breath-led stretches, slow joint circles, and a brief standing balance that cue alertness without overstimulation...

articles.title: A 4-4-6 Breath Cycle to Steady Anxiety
articles.excerpt: Learn a simple breathing pattern that you can pair with gentle movement for extra calm.
articles.content: The 4-4-6 breath is a slight variation on classic box breathing that lengthens the exhale to support your parasympathetic nervous system. In this article, we’ll break down the pattern, share common mistakes to avoid, and show you how to layer it onto seated twists and side bends to release upper body tension often stored with anxiety...

articles.title: Behind the 1-Minute Stress Check-In
articles.excerpt: How five simple questions help match you with the right movement for your day.
articles.content: Our stress check-in is designed to feel simple, but each question maps to a specific aspect of your nervous system: perceived stress, body tension, preferred movement, available time, and environment. In this article, we unpack why each question matters and how your answers shape the suggested sequences you see...

articles.title: Desk Breaks That Actually Reset Your Focus
articles.excerpt: Try these 3–5 minute movement snacks to counter stiffness and screen fatigue.
articles.content: Not all breaks are created equal. Scrolling your phone can add to cognitive load instead of easing it. In this piece, you’ll learn four movement ‘snacks’ designed for tiny windows in your workday. Each combines gentle mobility, breath, and gaze shifts to give your eyes and brain a chance to reset...

articles.title: Gentle Movement vs. Intense Workouts: What Your Nervous System Needs
articles.excerpt: Learn when to pick a calming class instead of another high-intensity workout.
articles.content: High-intensity training has its benefits, but your nervous system also needs slow, mindful sessions to recover. This guide walks you through signs you might be overdoing intensity and how to balance your week with gentle classes that still support strength and mobility...

articles.title: Mindfulness for Movers: A Beginner’s Guide
articles.excerpt: You don’t have to sit still to practice mindfulness. Start where you’re already moving.
articles.content: If sitting meditation feels intimidating, mindful movement can be a more accessible entry point. Here we outline a beginner-friendly approach to bringing awareness into everyday movements—like walking, stretching, and desk breaks—and how that relates to our class library...

articles.title: Why We Built a Mindful Movement App Instead of Another Gym Program
articles.excerpt: Our founders share how burnout and chronic stress reshaped their idea of fitness.
articles.content: After years in high-pressure careers, our founding team realized that the fitness industry rarely addressed how people actually felt in their bodies. This story walks through the personal experiences that led to creating a mindful movement platform focused on nervous system health, not just performance metrics...

articles.title: Rethinking Workplace Wellness: Tiny Breaks, Real Impact
articles.excerpt: How 5-minute movement breaks can reduce burnout and support focus for your team.
articles.content: Traditional wellness programs often overlook the power of short, frequent movement breaks. Drawing from new research and our work with remote teams, this article outlines how companies can weave micro-practices into the workday without disrupting productivity...

articles.title: A 5-Step Morning Routine to Lower Anxiety All Day
articles.excerpt: Set the tone for your nervous system before your to-do list takes over.
articles.content: Morning routines don’t have to be elaborate to be effective. In this guide, we propose a simple 5-step sequence—hydrate, light exposure, 5-minute mindful movement, intentional breathing, and a brief planning ritual—that can reduce baseline anxiety throughout your day...

articles.title: The Science of Naps and Nighttime Sleep
articles.excerpt: Learn how short daytime rest can support, not sabotage, your sleep.
articles.content: Naps are often blamed for poor nighttime sleep, but the relationship is more nuanced. This article reviews current research on nap length, timing, and individual differences, and offers movement-based strategies to transition from nap to wakefulness without grogginess...

articles.title: Breathwork You Can Do Between Meetings
articles.excerpt: Three discrete breathing practices that won’t make you feel self-conscious at work.
articles.content: You don’t need a meditation cushion or a dark room to benefit from breathwork. Here are three subtle techniques you can use in the office: paced nasal breathing, extended exhale, and dotted breath. Each can be practiced in under three minutes and paired with simple seated movements...

articles.title: Low-Impact Fitness That Still Builds Strength
articles.excerpt: Protect your joints while improving stability, balance, and muscle tone.
articles.content: Low-impact doesn’t mean low-challenge. In this guide, we explain how mindful, joint-friendly strength training can support your long-term movement practice. Expect sequences that alternate between slow, controlled strength work and restorative positions...

articles.title: Micro-Habits for Sustainable Movement
articles.excerpt: Build a routine that lasts by starting with tiny, repeatable actions.
articles.content: Sustainable movement routines rarely come from big resolutions. Instead, they grow from micro-habits—actions that are so small they’re almost impossible to skip. This article shows you how to use habit stacking and our reminder planner to build a 4-day movement rhythm that sticks...

articles.title: New: Offline Downloads and Smarter Reminders
articles.excerpt: Take your sessions on the go and fine-tune your reminder schedule with our latest update.
articles.content: We’ve rolled out two of our most-requested features: offline downloads and an upgraded reminder planner. In this news post, we walk through how to save sessions for offline use, what’s included at each plan level, and how to experiment with different reminder cadences...

instructors.name: Maya Lee
instructors.bio: Maya blends mindful movement, restorative yoga, and nervous system education to help students build calm, resilient bodies. She specializes in gentle sequences that fit into busy mornings and evenings.

instructors.name: Rafa Santos
instructors.bio: Rafa leads deeply restful restorative yoga sessions focused on breath, supported shapes, and long holds that invite the whole body to soften.

instructors.name: Ava Kim
instructors.bio: Ava teaches mindful movement sequences that weave in somatic awareness, playful balance work, and short desk-friendly practices.

instructors.name: Jamal Roberts
instructors.bio: Jamal focuses on low-impact fitness and mobility, helping students build strength that supports everyday life without overwhelming the nervous system.

instructors.name: Samira Chen
instructors.bio: Samira offers stretch & restore sessions perfect for late evenings, blending supported stretches with calming breathwork.

instructors.name: Amir Thompson
instructors.bio: Amir leads energizing flows that stay kind to your joints, balancing active sequences with grounding cooldowns.

instructors.name: Nadia Patel
instructors.bio: Nadia is a meditation and breathwork guide who specializes in short, approachable practices for beginners and busy professionals.

instructors.name: Chris O’Malley
instructors.bio: Chris designs short, desk-friendly movement snacks that help you shake off tension and refocus between tasks.

instructors.name: Lena Hofmann
instructors.bio: Lena combines restorative yoga and mindful breath to support deep rest and better sleep.

instructors.name: Omar García
instructors.bio: Omar focuses on breath-led mindful movement classes that support folks dealing with chronic stress and anxiety.

stress_questions.text: How stressed do you feel today?
How does your body feel right now?
What type of movement feels most supportive right now?
How much time do you have for movement today?
Where will you most likely do this session?

sessions.title: Gentle 15-Min Mat Reset
sessions.subtitle: Stress-soothing stretches you can do at home
sessions.description: A beginner-friendly, full-body reset you can do on a mat in your living room. Move through slow, floor-based stretches and simple breath cues designed to dial down anxiety and ease you into your day or evening.

sessions.title: Evening Stretch & Restore
sessions.subtitle: Unwind your body before bed
sessions.description: A slow, floor-based stretch & restore sequence to help you transition from your day into rest. Expect long-held, supported shapes and guided breathing to signal to your system that its time to wind down.

sessions.title: Desk Reset in 10
sessions.subtitle: Quick tension release between meetings
sessions.description: Shake off desk stiffness with this short, beginner-friendly sequence. Youll cycle through seated and standing moves that reset your posture, wake up circulation, and clear mental fog.

sessions.title: Soft Neck & Shoulders
sessions.subtitle: Release tension from screens and stress
sessions.description: Gentle, stretch & restore style work for your upper back, neck, and shoulders. Ideal after a long day at your desk or whenever you notice your jaw and shoulders creeping toward your ears.

sessions.title: 20-Min Midday Uncurl
sessions.subtitle: Reclaim your posture and focus
sessions.description: This gentle yet energizing sequence helps you uncurl from your screen, open your chest and hips, and return to your afternoon with more ease and clarity.

sessions.title: Mindful Morning Wake-Up
sessions.subtitle: Start your day with steadiness, not rush
sessions.description: An all-levels mindful movement flow that invites you to wake up your joints, connect to your breath, and set an intentional tone for the rest of your day.

sessions.title: Sunrise Restore for Calm
sessions.subtitle: A slow start for anxious mornings
sessions.description: Beginner restorative yoga on the mat with props or pillows. This session gently opens the body while inviting your breath and thoughts to slow, creating a calmer foundation for the rest of your day.

sessions.title: Breath & Body Balance
sessions.subtitle: 16 minutes to ground and re-center
sessions.description: A mat-based, beginner sequence that alternates simple stretches with breath-counting to help you reset when you feel scattered or overwhelmed.

sessions.title: Deep Restorative for Better Sleep
sessions.subtitle: Settle into longer holds before bed
sessions.description: A deeply restful restorative yoga class using blankets, pillows, or bolsters. Youll spend several minutes in each pose to encourage your body and mind to fully let go before sleep.

sessions.title: Grounded Power Flow
sessions.subtitle: Build heat without overwhelming your system
sessions.description: An advanced energizing flow that layers mindful pacing and intentional breath onto a strong standing sequence, helping you build strength while staying grounded.

sessions.title: 12-Min Seated Calm for Anxiety
sessions.subtitle: A guided meditation you can do anywhere
sessions.description: Stay seated as youre guided through grounding, breath awareness, and gentle body scanning to meet anxious energy with curiosity and care.

sessions.title: 8-Min Breath Reset at Your Desk
sessions.subtitle: Subtle breathwork you can do between tasks
sessions.description: Use simple, discreet breathing patterns to downshift from urgency and return to a steadier baseline, all without leaving your chair.

sessions.title: Evening Desk Release
sessions.subtitle: Gentle unwinding after work
sessions.description: Transition out of work mode with a gentle sequence focused on wrists, hips, and spine. Perfect after you close your laptop but before you move into your evening.

sessions.title: 10-Min Chair Softening
sessions.subtitle: Wind down without getting off the couch
sessions.description: Stay seated as you move through gentle side bends, twists, and forward folds, paired with slow breathing to cue your body that its safe to rest.

sessions.title: Low-Impact Foundation Flow
sessions.subtitle: Build strength that supports everyday life
sessions.description: An accessible, low-impact strength and stability session that alternates slow, controlled work with brief rest, keeping your joints happy while you build capacity.

sessions.title: 15-Min Legs-Up-the-Wall Reset
sessions.subtitle: Soothe your nervous system before sleep
sessions.description: Move through a short sequence that leads into a supported legs-up-the-wall pose, using simple breath counting to encourage a deep sense of calm.

sessions.title: Slow Flow to Steady the Mind
sessions.subtitle: Mindful movement for anxious evenings
sessions.description: A gentle, breath-led standing and mat flow designed to meet anxiety with movement, then guide you toward a steadier, more grounded state.

sessions.title: Morning Focus Meditation
sessions.subtitle: Set a calm, clear tone for your workday
sessions.description: A 15-minute guided meditation that combines breath awareness and light visualization to help you shift from groggy to grounded before you open your inbox.

suggested_plans.name: Calm in 15: Gentle Mat Reset
suggested_plans.description: A short, mat-based reset for high-stress days. Start with a gentle full-body sequence to discharge anxious energy, then choose an optional seated meditation if you have a few extra minutes.

suggested_plans.name: High-Stress Evening Sleep Support
suggested_plans.description: For nights when your mind wont switch off. Move through deeply restorative shapes and a simple inversion to signal safety and prepare your body for sleep.

suggested_plans.name: Workday Resets for a Steadier Week
suggested_plans.description: A sequence of desk-friendly resets to sprinkle through your workweek. Use these sessions to release tension, clear mental fog, and transition out of work mode with more ease.

suggested_plans.name: Low-Stress Morning Energy Builder
suggested_plans.description: On days when stress is low but energy is fuzzy, use this mix of mindful movement, low-impact strength, and calm focus meditation to start clear and steady.

suggested_plans.name: Evening Ease for Anxious Minds
suggested_plans.description: When stress is medium but your thoughts are busy, this plan offers gentle evening flows, upper-body release, and a short meditation to help you land.
